summarization.a: The figure shows the change in MCPC (mass change per cycle) and ΔM (mass change due to HF and TMA) over cycles at 300°C on Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>. The values remain relative constant, ΔM values have an outlier (first cycle).
summarization.b: The figure displays the ratio of ΔM<sub>TMA</sub> to MCPC over cycles. The value remains relatively constant.

a.question: Which step produces the larger mass change?
What do the values of  ΔM<sub>TMA</sub> and ΔM<sub>HF</sub> imply about what each reactant is doing during the ALE cycle?
a.answer: A positive ΔM<sub>HF</sub> means the HF step increases the surface mass, consisting with fluorinating the surface. A negative ΔM<sub>TMA</sub> means the TMA step decreases the surface mass, indicating it removes material by converting the surface species into volatile products that leave the surface. Overall, HF builds a fluorinated layer, and TMA strips it.

b.question: Does the ALE process appear to be in steady state between cycles 40 and 80?
